name: Auñón to the Sanctuary of the Virgen del Madroñal Trail
name_original: Santuario de la Virgen del Madroñal — circular a orillas del embalse de Entrepeñas
description: Hike a moderate 7.1-mile (11.4 km) circular route to the Santuario de la Virgen del Madroñal, with panoramic views of Entrepeñas reservoir.
region_intro: Hiking around Auñón offers routes through the Alcarria Baja region of Guadalajara, characterized by scenic landscapes and proximity to significant natural features. The area is defined by the Tagus River and the Embalse de Bolarque, providing opportunities for waterside trails. Hikers can explore varied terrain, including paths that are part of the extensive Tajo Natural Path. Best hiking trails around Auñón The most popular hiking route is Ermita de Nuestra Señora de los Desamparados – circular along the Guadiela River (PR-CU 47), a 8.0 miles (12.8 km) trail that takes 3 hours 27 minutes to complete. This moderate route follows the Guadiela River, offering riverside views. Another top favourite among local hikers is Camino del Socorro Trail, a moderate 8.7 miles (13.9 km) path. This trail features an elevation gain of over 1,400 feet (438 meters) and traverses varied landscapes. Local hikers also love the Auñón to the Sanctuary of the Virgen del Madroñal Trail, a 7.1 miles (11.4 km) trail leading through the local countryside towards a notable sanctuary, often completed in about 3 hours 21 minutes. Hiking around Auñón is defined by the Tagus River, the Embalse de Bolarque, and the Alcarria Baja region. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes. The routes in Auñón are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 30 reviews. More than 300 hikers have used komoot to explore Auñón's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Auñón?
A: There are &lt;strong&gt;over 15 hiking trails&lt;/strong&gt; around Auñón, offering a variety of experiences for different skill levels. These include 6 easy routes, 8 moderate routes, and 1 difficult route, ensuring options for everyone from casual walkers to experienced hikers.

Q: Are there any easy, family-friendly hiking routes in Auñón?
A: Yes, Auñón offers several easy routes suitable for families. One notable option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31443085" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the Faces loop from Aparcamiento Ruta de Las Caras&lt;/a&gt;, which is an easy 1.5 km trail. This area is known for its unique carved faces, making it an engaging walk for all ages.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see on hikes near Auñón?
A: Hiking around Auñón offers diverse landscapes, primarily defined by its proximity to the &lt;strong&gt;Tagus River&lt;/strong&gt; and the &lt;strong&gt;Embalse de Bolarque&lt;/strong&gt;. You can expect scenic waterside trails, paths through the Alcarria Baja region, and varied terrain that is part of the extensive Tajo Natural Path. The area combines natural beauty with cultural heritage.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in the Auñón area?
A: Yes, there are circular routes available. A popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1012673371/ermita-de-nuestra-senora-de-los-desamparados-circular-along-the-guadiela-river-pr-cu-47" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ermita de Nuestra Señora de los Desamparados – circular along the Guadiela River (PR-CU 47)&lt;/a&gt;. This moderate 12.8 km trail follows the Guadiela River, providing pleasant riverside views.

Q: What are some interesting landmarks or points of interest to explore along the trails?
A: Beyond the natural beauty, Auñón's trails offer access to several interesting landmarks. You can visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4313970" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanctuary of the Virgen del Madroñal&lt;/a&gt;, which is the destination of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1025793863/aunon-to-the-sanctuary-of-the-virgen-del-madronal-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Auñón to the Sanctuary of the Virgen del Madroñal Trail&lt;/a&gt;. Other notable attractions include the unique &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3905216" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the Faces&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6503478" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bridge over the Guadiela River&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any viewpoints with panoramic views near Auñón's hiking trails?
A: Yes, the region offers several viewpoints. You can find scenic vistas at the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5398646" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña de la Virgen Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4440584" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Entrepeñas Dam Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, both offering expansive views of the surrounding lakes and natural features.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Auñón?
A: The trails in Auñón are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an average rating of &lt;strong&gt;4.6 out of 5 stars&lt;/strong&gt; from over 35 reviews. Hikers often praise the varied terrain, the beautiful riverside paths, and the opportunity to combine natural exploration with visits to historical sites.

Q: Are there any trails that feature significant elevation gain for a more challenging hike?
A: For those seeking more challenging ascents, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1025777281/camino-del-socorro-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camino del Socorro Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 13.9 km path that features an elevation gain of over 438 meters, traversing varied landscapes and offering a good workout.

Q: Can I combine hiking with exploring the town's cultural heritage?
A: Absolutely. Auñón itself is a beautiful town with significant monumental heritage. Hikers can explore historical sites such as the Trasconvento, the Hermitage of La Soledad, the Church, the chapel of the Bishop of Salona, and various manor houses, adding a rich cultural dimension to your outdoor excursions.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Auñón?
A: While specific seasonal details are not provided, the region's characteristics suggest that spring and autumn would be ideal for hiking in Auñón. These seasons typically offer milder temperatures and pleasant conditions for exploring the riverside paths and varied terrain without the extreme heat of summer or potential chill of winter.

Q: Are there any trails that follow the Tagus River or are near the Embalse de Bolarque?
A: Yes, the region's hiking opportunities are closely tied to the &lt;strong&gt;Tagus River&lt;/strong&gt; and the &lt;strong&gt;Embalse de Bolarque&lt;/strong&gt;. Many routes, including sections of the extensive Tajo Natural Path, offer waterside trails and picturesque views of the reservoir and river, providing a unique natural experience.
tips: There are amazing places in Guadalajara even for the people of this land. This route (Route SPG-47) takes you to one of those places, the Hermitage of the Virgen del Madroñal, a mystical environment with a privileged location on the waters of the Entrepeñas Reservoir.

faqs.question: How long does this hike typically take to complete?
faqs.answer: This circular route is approximately 14.5 km long and generally takes between 4 to 5 hours to complete, depending on your pace and stops.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this trail?
faqs.answer: The trail is considered to have an easy to moderate difficulty. However, be prepared for a challenging initial climb towards Pico Tejero, which involves a significant elevation gain of nearly 300 meters. After this ascent, the rest of the route is more manageable.

faqs.question: Where does the trail start, and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The trail typically starts from the town of Auñón. While the sources don't specify dedicated parking, Auñón is the common starting point, suggesting parking would be available within the town or nearby.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the Santuario de la Virgen del Madroñal trail?
faqs.answer: The provided information does not specify restrictions on dogs. It's generally advisable to keep dogs on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or near historical sites, and to follow local regulations.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: The best seasons to undertake this hike are spring and autumn. These times offer more pleasant temperatures for the initial climb and enjoying the panoramic views.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this hike?
faqs.answer: No, there is no information indicating that a permit or entrance fee is required to hike this trail or visit the Santuario de la Virgen del Madroñal.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this circular route?
faqs.answer: You'll encounter diverse terrain, including a notable ascent on paths towards Pico Tejero, clearings within pine forests, and potentially some asphalted sections, particularly on the return journey to Auñón. The path offers a mix of natural trails and paved surfaces.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest or viewpoints along the way?
faqs.answer: The route offers stunning natural scenery and several notable points. You'll pass through extensive pine forests and enjoy breathtaking elevated views of the Entrepeñas reservoir, often called the 'Sea of Castile'. From various points, you can observe the 'Boca del Infierno' (Hell's Mouth) of the reservoir and the distant houses of Sacedón. The highlight is the Sanctuary of the Virgen del Madroñal itself, a 17th-century hermitage perched on a cliffside overlooking the reservoir.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with any other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, parts of this route follow sections of the GR 113, which is also known as the Camino Natural del Tajo.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for families or children?
faqs.answer: While generally accessible, the initial climb towards Pico Tejero is quite demanding. Families with younger children or those less accustomed to significant ascents might find this section challenging. The trail is better suited for those with a good fitness level.

faqs.question: What is the strategic tip for tackling the initial climb on this route?
faqs.answer: The most demanding part of the route is the initial ascent towards Pico Tejero. It's advisable to start this section at a comfortable pace, especially if hiking in warmer weather, and ensure you have sufficient water. Once you reach Pico Tejero, the path becomes easier as it descends towards the hermitage.

waypoints.name: Sanctuary of the Virgen del Madroñal
